policy.title: RBI Finalizes Climate Risk Stress Testing Guidelines for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI issued final guidelines mandating SCBs with assets over ₹1 lakh crore to conduct annual climate stress tests starting FY2027, with physical risk PD adjustments required for agriculture and MSME portfolios. Banks must submit baseline scenario results by September 2026.

policy.title: SEBI Extends BRSR Core Assurance to Top 500 Listed Companies
policy.description: SEBI mandated reasonable assurance on BRSR Core metrics for top 500 companies by market cap effective FY2026-27, expanding from top 150. New requirements include Scope 3 emissions disclosure for material categories.

policy.title: IRDAI Issues Draft Framework for Index-Based Climate Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI released draft regulations permitting insurers to offer parametric covers triggered by IITM/IMD certified weather indices, with maximum payout limits of ₹50 lakh for commercial lines. Public comments due by 15 April 2026.

policy.title: Basel Committee Publishes Final Pillar 3 Climate Risk Disclosure Standards
policy.description: BCBS finalized Pillar 3 disclosure requirements for climate-related financial risks, mandating quantitative metrics on financed emissions and physical risk exposures by January 2028 for G-SIBs, with phased rollout to other banks.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Pathways
policy.description: NGFS published updated scenarios incorporating IPCC AR6 WG1 findings, with new chronic physical risk variables for South Asia including compound heat-drought indices. Scenarios now extend to 2100 with decadal granularity.

policy.title: EFRAG Finalizes Sector-Specific ESRS for Financial Institutions
policy.description: EFRAG adopted sector standards requiring banks and insurers to disclose transition plan alignment with 1.5°C pathways, portfolio emission intensity metrics, and physical risk materiality assessments by asset class starting FY2027.

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Raises $100M Series D for Global Expansion
competitors.description: Jupiter closed $100M Series D led by TPG Rise Climate to expand physical risk analytics into emerging markets, specifically citing India, Brazil, and Southeast Asia. Company valuation reached $650M post-money.

competitors.title: GIST Advisory Partners with HDFC Bank for Natural Capital Risk Assessment
competitors.description: GIST announced a multi-year engagement with HDFC Bank to integrate natural capital dependencies into credit risk frameworks, covering agricultural and textiles portfolios worth ₹85,000 crore.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches India-Specific Physical Risk Scores for Bank Portfolios
competitors.description: Moody's introduced district-level physical risk scores covering 780 Indian districts across six perils, integrated with their credit rating methodology. Product targets Indian banks seeking RBI stress test compliance.

competitors.title: Sprih Secures $8M Series A for Enterprise Decarbonization Platform
competitors.description: Bengaluru-based Sprih raised $8M led by Accel India to expand its carbon accounting SaaS targeting mid-market Indian enterprises. Platform now covers Scope 1-3 with BRSR-aligned reporting templates.

competitors.title: XDI Partners with S&P Global for Physical Risk Data Integration
competitors.description: XDI's asset-level physical risk data will be integrated into S&P Global's Climanomics platform, providing flood, cyclone, and heat stress projections for 400 million global assets including significant India coverage.

competitors.title: Watershed Launches Financial Services Module with Portfolio Emissions
competitors.description: Watershed released dedicated FI functionality supporting PCAF-aligned financed emissions calculations across seven asset classes, targeting banks preparing for ISSB S2 compliance. India go-to-market planned for Q3 2026.

research.title: Climate Risk and Credit: Quantifying Physical Risk Transmission to Bank Portfolios
research.description: BIS working paper finds that a 1-standard-deviation increase in physical risk exposure correlates with 12-18bps higher PD for Indian MSME borrowers, with effects concentrated in monsoon-dependent districts. Study covers 2.3 million loan observations from 2015-2024.

research.title: Parametric Solutions for Climate Risk Transfer in South Asia
research.description: Report estimates India's parametric insurance protection gap at $12 billion annually, with agriculture and SME credit guarantee sectors showing highest demand. Recommends hybrid products combining index triggers with traditional coverage.

research.title: ESG Data Gaps in Emerging Markets: Implications for Sustainable Finance
research.description: IFC analysis reveals only 23% of Indian listed companies provide decision-useful climate data, dropping to 8% for unlisted MSMEs. Recommends proxy-based methodologies and alternative data sources for portfolio assessment.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk Assessment for Indian Banking System
research.description: CRISIL estimates ₹8.2 lakh crore of Indian bank exposure faces elevated physical risk, with 34% of agricultural advances in high-vulnerability districts. Report provides state-wise risk heatmaps across five climate perils.

research.title: Supervisory Approaches to Climate Scenario Analysis: Lessons from Early Adopters
research.description: FSB review of 28 jurisdictional climate stress tests finds significant divergence in physical risk modeling approaches, with only 5 jurisdictions incorporating acute-chronic risk interactions. Calls for methodological convergence.

research.title: Index-Based Insurance and Agricultural Resilience in India
research.description: Impact evaluation of PMFBY and pilot parametric schemes finds index-based products reduce basis risk by 40% when combined with satellite vegetation indices. Recommends rainfall-NDVI composite triggers for kharif crops.

ourRead.headline: RBI's stress test mandate creates a compliance deadline that plays directly to StepChange's core capability—but Moody's India push signals the window for first-mover advantage is narrowing fast.
body: The signals this fortnight converge on a single theme: Indian financial regulators are transitioning from voluntary frameworks to hard compliance timelines, while global competitors recognize India as the next frontier for climate risk products. The RBI stress testing mandate, SEBI BRSR expansion, and IRDAI parametric draft collectively create the regulatory pull that StepChange has been positioning for—but the competitive intensity is rising faster than anticipated.

The RBI's finalized climate stress testing guidelines are the most consequential policy development. With SCBs over ₹1 lakh crore now required to submit physical risk PD adjustments by September 2026, banks face an 18-month scramble to build or buy analytical capability. The specific requirement for agriculture and MSME portfolio stress tests aligns precisely with StepChange's district-level hazard data strengths. The BIS working paper quantifying 12-18bps PD uplift from physical risk provides the empirical validation banks need to justify methodology choices—StepChange should incorporate these benchmarks into client conversations immediately.

Moody's India-specific physical risk launch represents the most significant competitive threat. Their 780-district coverage and explicit RBI compliance positioning directly challenges StepChange's differentiation. However, Moody's product appears oriented toward disclosure compliance rather than credit decisioning—the GIST-HDFC partnership suggests sophisticated Indian banks want deeper natural capital integration, not just hazard scores. Jupiter's $100M raise with explicit India expansion plans adds urgency. StepChange's edge lies in proprietary local calibration and parametric product integration that global players cannot easily replicate; the next two quarters must lock in anchor bank relationships before Jupiter operationalizes their India entry.

The Swiss Re parametric report quantifying India's $12 billion protection gap and recommending hybrid index-traditional products is a direct brief for StepChange's second strategic bet. Combined with IRDAI's draft framework permitting IMD-certified weather triggers, the path to scalable parametric products is clearer than ever. The World Bank research on NDVI-rainfall composite triggers provides the technical blueprint—StepChange should accelerate development of these hybrid indices for IRDAI pilot submissions.

Watch: IRDAI's April 15 comment deadline is the near-term regulatory moment; StepChange should submit detailed technical comments to shape the final framework. Action: Prioritize a joint response paper with a progressive insurer partner that positions StepChange as the data backbone for compliant parametric products—this is the fastest path to embedded market position before the framework finalizes.
